description: One-pass review of crate secretx-systemd (secretx-systemd/). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — the only meaningful risk is the validation gap (0hfd.1/.2), which currently produces confusing error messages rather than security exposure because systemd's namespacing covers the data-leakage vector in production.
Correctness confidence: 90% no missed bug. Least confident flags: behaviour when CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY points to a path containing symlinks (we follow them; systemd does not create them, so this is a defence-in-depth only matter); behaviour when an attacker controls both the URI and CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY (out of threat model). Uncertainty driven by: not having instrumented systemd's actual creds tmpfs in a live VM to verify namespace isolation independently; reliance on systemd source-code review (creds-util.c:46) rather than running its tests. Would raise confidence with: a host-namespace integration test, and an internal table of all URIs that pass parse() but should fail.
Idiom rating: CLEAN — clippy --all-features -- -D warnings passes, RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc passes, typos clean, no unsafe outside tests, all idiomatic patterns followed. Two non-blocking idiom findings (0hfd.8 const for backend name, 0hfd.9 tokio::fs vs spawn_blocking) are workspace-consistency issues, not crate-internal idiom failures.
Rust idiom confidence: 95% no missed issue. Least confident flags: none — the crate is small (264 lines incl. tests) and the surface is narrow. Uncertainty driven by: did not exhaustively test every clippy lint group (cargo clippy was run with default lints + -D warnings, not pedantic/nursery/restriction). Filing 0hfd.8 and 0hfd.9 as workspace-pattern findings rather than crate-blocking issues.
title: Review: secretx-systemd rusty pass
